Burial chamber: west wall offering-text and offerings; niche with palace-façade decoration and titles. Monuments of deceased: fragments of false-door; wooden statue, uninscribed, found wrapped in linen, in Cairo Mus. JE 59631. Other finds: offering-tables of Niʿankh-khons, Neferi, Wepti, Seni (Director of the dining hall), and one dedicated by son Pepyemḥet; five shells with hieratic texts; obelisk of Idu-tepiḥu, King's eldest son, probably from another tomb.
